message:
type: SURVEY
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Implementing multi-file support for click.edit(): renaming Editor.edit_file() to edit_files() (accepts iterable of filenames), updating edit() in termui.py to accept filename: str | Iterable[str] | None with overloads

type: SURVEY
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Adding process_priority param to click.edit() and Editor class. Touches Editor.__init__ (add process_priority attr), Editor.edit_file (apply priority to subprocess.Popen), and termui.edit() function signature/docstring + Editor(...) instantiation call.

type: DRAFT
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py
region: Editor.__init__
code:     def __init__(
        self,
        editor: str | None = None,
        env: cabc.Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
        require_save: bool = True,
        extension: str = ".txt",
        process_priority: int = 0,
    ) -> None:
        self.editor = editor
        self.env = env
        self.require_save = require_save
        self.extension = extension
        self.process_priority = process_priority
note: Editor.__init__ merged: my multi-file change doesn't touch __init__ at all, so this is basically your process_priority addition verbatim. Please correct type/default if I guessed wrong (I used int=0 for 'no change' semantics).

type: DRAFT
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py
region: Editor.edit_file -> edit_files method
code:     def edit_files(self, filenames: cabc.Iterable[str]) -> None:
        import subprocess

        editor = self.get_editor()
        environ: dict[str, str] | None = None

        if self.env:
            environ = os.environ.copy()
            environ.update(self.env)

        exc_filenames = " ".join(f'"{filename}"' for filename in filenames)

        try:
            c = subprocess.Popen(
                f"{editor} {exc_filenames}",
                env=environ,
                shell=True,
            )
            exit_code = c.wait()
            if exit_code != 0:
                raise ClickException(
                    _("{editor}: Editing failed").format(editor=editor)
                )
        except OSError as e:
            raise ClickException(
                _("{editor}: Editing failed: {e}").format(editor=editor, e=e)
            ) from e
note: Renamed edit_file->edit_files, accepts iterable of filenames, joins them as separate quoted args. Does NOT yet include process_priority usage in the Popen call -- please tell me exactly how/where you apply self.process_priority (e.g. preexec_fn=lambda: os.nice(...) or subprocess creationflags) so I can merge it in verbatim.

type: DRAFT
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py
region: Editor.edit_file/edit_files method
code: def edit_files(self, filenames: cabc.Iterable[str]) -> None:
    import subprocess

    editor = self.get_editor()
    environ: dict[str, str] | None = None

    if self.env:
        environ = os.environ.copy()
        environ.update(self.env)

    exc_filenames = " ".join(f'"{filename}"' for filename in filenames)

    try:
        c = subprocess.Popen(
            f"{editor} {exc_filenames}",
            env=environ,
            shell=True,
            **self._priority_popen_kwargs(),
        )
        self._apply_process_priority(c.pid)
        exit_code = c.wait()
        if exit_code != 0:
            raise ClickException(
                _("{editor}: Editing failed").format(editor=editor)
            )
    except OSError as e:
        raise ClickException(
            _("{editor}: Editing failed: {e}").format(editor=editor, e=e)
        ) from e
note: Renamed per your SURVEY to accept Iterable[str]. I added two helper calls (_priority_popen_kwargs for Windows creationflags, _apply_process_priority for POSIX os.setpriority after start) that I will define elsewhere in the class (non-overlapping, safe to ignore on your side). Please confirm this signature/body matches your plan for multi-file support, or send your version and I'll fold in the priority hooks.

note: Correcting type: process_priority must be str | None (values: None/low/below_normal/normal/above_normal/high per spec), default None -- not int=0. Please use this exact version.

files: src/click/_termui_impl.py
region: Editor.edit_file -> edit_files method
code:     def edit_files(self, filenames: cabc.Iterable[str]) -> None:
        import subprocess

        editor = self.get_editor()
        environ: dict[str, str] | None = None

        if self.env:
            environ = os.environ.copy()
            environ.update(self.env)

        exc_filenames = " ".join(f'"{filename}"' for filename in filenames)

        try:
            c = subprocess.Popen(
                f"{editor} {exc_filenames}",
                env=environ,
                shell=True,
                **self._priority_popen_kwargs(),
            )
            self._apply_process_priority(c.pid)
            exit_code = c.wait()
            if exit_code != 0:
                raise ClickException(
                    _("{editor}: Editing failed").format(editor=editor)
                )
        except OSError as e:
            raise ClickException(
                _("{editor}: Editing failed: {e}").format(editor=editor, e=e)
            ) from e
note: Adds two hook calls: _priority_popen_kwargs() returns {} unless process_priority set and on Windows (adds creationflags); _apply_process_priority(pid) sets os.setpriority on POSIX after the process starts, swallowing OSError so a priority failure never breaks editing. Both helper methods are added by me elsewhere in the class body (non-overlapping) -- I'll paste their exact code next for your reference, but you don't need to add them since I own that region.

type: DRAFT
files: src/click/termui.py
region: edit() function
code: def edit(
    text: t.AnyStr | None = None,
    editor: str | None = None,
    env: cabc.Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
    require_save: bool = True,
    extension: str = ".txt",
    filename: str | cabc.Iterable[str] | None = None,
    process_priority: str | None = None,
) -> t.AnyStr | None:
    r"""Edits the given text in the defined editor.  If an editor is given
    (should be the full path to the executable but the regular operating
    system search path is used for finding the executable) it overrides
    the detected editor.  Optionally, some environment variables can be
    used.  If the editor is closed without changes, `None` is returned.  In
    case a file is edited directly the return value is always `None` and
    `require_save` and `extension` are ignored.

    If the editor cannot be opened a :exc:`UsageError` is raised.

    Note for Windows: to simplify cross-platform usage, the newlines are
    automatically converted from POSIX to Windows and vice versa.  As such,
    the message here will have ``\n`` as newline markers.

    :param text: the text to edit.
    :param editor: optionally the editor to use.  Defaults to automatic
                   detection.
    :param env: environment variables to forward to the editor.
    :param require_save: if this is true, then not saving in the editor
                         will make the return value become `None`.
    :param extension: the extension to tell the editor about.  This defaults
                      to `.txt` but changing this might change syntax
                      highlighting.
    :param filename: if provided it will edit this file instead of the
                     provided text contents.  It will not use a temporary
                     file as an indirection in that case.  If a list or
                     iterable of filenames is given, all of them are
                     opened for editing at the same time in the editor,
                     for editors that support this.
    :param process_priority: optionally set the priority of the editor
                             process.  One of ``"low"``, ``"below_normal"``,
                             ``"normal"``, ``"above_normal"``, ``"high"``.
                             Defaults to the operating system default.

    .. versionchanged:: 8.2.0
        The ``filename`` parameter can now be a list of filenames to
        edit multiple files at once.

    .. versionchanged:: 8.2.0
        Added the ``process_priority`` parameter.
    """
    from ._termui_impl import Editor

    ed = Editor(
        editor=editor,
        env=env,
        require_save=require_save,
        extension=extension,
        process_priority=process_priority,
    )

    if filename is None:
        return ed.edit(text)

    if isinstance(filename, str):
        filenames = (filename,)
    else:
        filenames = tuple(filename)

    ed.edit_files(filenames)
    return None
note: Merged edit(): filename now accepts str|Iterable[str]|None (multi-file), plus your process_priority: str|None param threaded into Editor(...) and docstring. I still need to add @t.overload signatures above this for backward-compat typing (non-overlapping, I'll add those myself). Let me know if process_priority docstring/versionchanged wording needs adjusting.

type: DRAFT
files: src/click/termui.py
region: edit() function
code: def edit(
    text: t.AnyStr | None = None,
    editor: str | None = None,
    env: cabc.Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
    require_save: bool = True,
    extension: str = ".txt",
    filename: str | cabc.Iterable[str] | None = None,
    process_priority: str | None = None,
) -> t.AnyStr | None:
    r"""Edits the given text in the defined editor.  If an editor is given
    (should be the full path to the executable but the regular operating
    system search path is used for finding the executable) it overrides
    the detected editor.  Optionally, some environment variables can be
    used.  If the editor is closed without changes, `None` is returned.  In
    case a file is edited directly the return value is always `None` and
    `require_save` and `extension` are ignored.

    If the editor cannot be opened a :exc:`UsageError` is raised.

    Note for Windows: to simplify cross-platform usage, the newlines are
    automatically converted from POSIX to Windows and vice versa.  As such,
    the message here will have ``\n`` as newline markers.

    :param text: the text to edit.
    :param editor: optionally the editor to use.  Defaults to automatic
                   detection.
    :param env: environment variables to forward to the editor.
    :param require_save: if this is true, then not saving in the editor
                         will make the return value become `None`.
    :param extension: the extension to tell the editor about.  This defaults
                      to `.txt` but changing this might change syntax
                      highlighting.
    :param filename: if provided it will edit this file (or, given an
                     iterable of filenames, these files together) instead
                     of the provided text contents.  It will not use a
                     temporary file as an indirection in that case.
    :param process_priority: optionally set the CPU priority of the editor
                             process.  One of ``"low"``, ``"below_normal"``,
                             ``"normal"``, ``"above_normal"``, ``"high"``.
                             Defaults to ``None``, which leaves the process
                             at normal system priority.  Silently ignored
                             if unsupported on the current platform.
    """
    from ._termui_impl import Editor

    ed = Editor(
        editor=editor,
        env=env,
        require_save=require_save,
        extension=extension,
        process_priority=process_priority,
    )

    if filename is None:
        return ed.edit(text)

    if isinstance(filename, str):
        ed.edit_files([filename])
    else:
        ed.edit_files(filename)

    return None
note: Merged: filename now accepts str | Iterable[str] | None per your SURVEY, plus process_priority param for mine. I did not add @t.overload stubs -- if you need them for typing, please add them above this def and I will adopt whatever you send back verbatim (they would not conflict with this body).

type: AGREE

note: Implemented all three agreed constructs verbatim (Editor.__init__, edit_files, edit()). Also updated the self.edit_file(name) call inside Editor.edit() to self.edit_files([name]) since edit_file no longer exists -- necessary either way after the rename, so should be identical if you make the same change. Added module-level _POSIX_NICE_VALUES/_WIN32_PRIORITY_CLASSES dicts and two new non-overlapping helper methods (_priority_popen_kwargs, _apply_process_priority) placed between __init__ and get_editor. Tests pass (tests/ -k edit). Done on my end.

    :param require_save: if this is true, then not saving in the editor
                         will make the return value become `None`.
    :param extension: the extension to tell the editor about.  This defaults
                      to `.txt` but changing this might change syntax
                      highlighting.
    :param filename: if provided it will edit this file (or, given an
                     iterable of filenames, these files together) instead
                     of the provided text contents.  It will not use a
                     temporary file as an indirection in that case.
    :param process_priority: optionally set the CPU priority of the editor
                             process.  One of ``"low"``, ``"below_normal"``,
                             ``"normal"``, ``"above_normal"``, ``"high"``.
                             Defaults to ``None``, which leaves the process
                             at normal system priority.  Silently ignored
                             if unsupported on the current platform.

    .. versionchanged:: 8.2.0
        The ``filename`` parameter can now also be an iterable of
        filenames to edit multiple files at once.

    .. versionchanged:: 8.2.0
        Added the ``process_priority`` parameter.
    """
    from ._termui_impl import Editor

    ed = Editor(
        editor=editor,
        env=env,
        require_save=require_save,
        extension=extension,
        process_priority=process_priority,
    )

    if filename is None:
        return ed.edit(text)

    if isinstance(filename, str):
        ed.edit_files([filename])
    else:
        ed.edit_files(filename)

    return None

code: def edit(
    text: t.AnyStr | None = None,
    editor: str | None = None,
    env: cabc.Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
    require_save: bool = True,
    extension: str = ".txt",
    filename: str | cabc.Iterable[str] | None = None,
    process_priority: str | None = None,
) -> t.AnyStr | None:
    r"""Edits the given text in the defined editor.  If an editor is given
    (should be the full path to the executable but the regular operating
    system search path is used for finding the executable) it overrides
    the detected editor.  Optionally, some environment variables can be
    used.  If the editor is closed without changes, `None` is returned.  In
    case a file is edited directly the return value is always `None` and
    `require_save` and `extension` are ignored.

    If the editor cannot be opened a :exc:`UsageError` is raised.

    Note for Windows: to simplify cross-platform usage, the newlines are
    automatically converted from POSIX to Windows and vice versa.  As such,
    the message here will have ``\n`` as newline markers.

    :param text: the text to edit.
    :param editor: optionally the editor to use.  Defaults to automatic
                   detection.
    :param env: environment variables to forward to the editor.
    :param require_save: if this is true, then not saving in the editor
                         will make the return value become `None`.
    :param extension: the extension to tell the editor about.  This defaults
                      to `.txt` but changing this might change syntax
                      highlighting.
    :param filename: if provided it will edit this file (or, given an
                     iterable of filenames, these files together) instead
                     of the provided text contents.  It will not use a
                     temporary file as an indirection in that case.
    :param process_priority: optionally set the CPU priority of the editor
                             process.  One of ``"low"``, ``"below_normal"``,
                             ``"normal"``, ``"above_normal"``, ``"high"``.
                             Defaults to ``None``, which leaves the process
                             at normal system priority.  Silently ignored
                             if unsupported on the current platform.

    .. versionchanged:: 8.2.0
        The ``filename`` parameter can now also be an iterable of
        filenames to edit multiple files at once.

    .. versionchanged:: 8.2.0
        Added the ``process_priority`` parameter.
    """
    from ._termui_impl import Editor

    ed = Editor(
        editor=editor,
        env=env,
        require_save=require_save,
        extension=extension,
        process_priority=process_priority,
    )

    if filename is None:
        return ed.edit(text)

    if isinstance(filename, str):
        ed.edit_files([filename])
    else:
        ed.edit_files(filename)

    return None
